criteria.description: Evidence of deliberate harm: data exfiltration, backdoors, credential theft, or hidden functionality contradicting the stated purpose. Score 10 if no indicators exist; list any concrete indicator with its file reference in risk_flags.

Encoded payloads, eval/exec of strings, dynamic code construction, base64 or hex blobs hiding behavior. Benign uses (e.g. data encoding with clear purpose) should not lower the score much; unexplained obfuscation should. 10 = none found.

Outbound calls to unexpected or hardcoded destinations: raw IP URLs, unexplained POST/PUT endpoints, domains unrelated to the project's stated purpose. 10 = all network activity clearly matches the project's purpose.
Destructive or system-level file operations: deleting system paths, modifying system configs or the registry, world-writable permissions, piping remote scripts to a shell. 10 = none found.

Judge the declared dependency list for typosquatting (near-misses of popular package names), suspicious or unnecessary packages, and unpinned versions. Note: no CVE database was consulted — reason from the names and versions given. 10 = clean, pinned, well-known packages.

Code that runs at install time: npm preinstall/postinstall/prepare scripts, setup.py custom commands, Makefile remote pipes. Any install hook executing network fetches or shells is a strong indicator. 10 = no install-time execution.

Scheduled tasks whose purpose is unclear or that execute remote content. Legitimate documented schedules (e.g. a daily data refresh described in the README) should score well. 10 = no schedules or all clearly legitimate.
Container risks: privileged mode, ADD from remote URLs, remote scripts piped to shell during build, ENTRYPOINT fetching remote code. 10 = no container files or no risky patterns.
